title: Civilian Deaths in the NATO Air Campaign
pages_or_sections: summary section; lines 47-51 in tool view
strengths: Widely cited incident-based civilian casualty review with explicit low and high counts and geographic distribution.
limitations: Covers civilian deaths only; not a complete same-framework military casualty or denominator source.
notes: Primary civilian numerator source for the campaign-level episode.

title: Under Orders: War Crimes in Kosovo - 3. Forces of the Conflict
pages_or_sections: section 3; lines 31-37 and 115-116 in tool views
strengths: Provides bounded numbers for Yugoslav Army personnel and police/special-police formations and situates them in the 1999 theater.
limitations: Force-size discussion is not a same-day campaign roster and is strongest for Kosovo rather than every FRY bombing zone.
notes: Used to recover a bounded military exposure floor rather than a precise campaign-wide census.

title: Kosovo and Macedonia: U.S. and Allied Military Operations
pages_or_sections: pp. 4-5; lines 152-155 and 188-199 in tool view
strengths: Explains campaign timing and target geography, and notes movement of Yugoslav Army and MUP forces during the campaign.
notes: Used to justify the 78-day campaign window and the campaign-wide theater logic.

title: Serbia marks 14 years since NATO bombing
author_or_org: B92 citing Tanjug and Serbian official commemorative reporting
pages_or_sections: main text; lines 140-149 in tool view
strengths: Provides a specific retrospective partition of 1,008 military and police deaths into 659 soldiers and 349 policemen.
limitations: Retrospective commemorative source rather than a contemporaneous transparent casualty database; may reflect official memory politics.
notes: Used as the best recoverable same-campaign military/police death figure, with limitations stated openly.

title: Federal Republic of Yugoslavia (Serbia and Montenegro) - population estimate
author_or_org: Central Intelligence Agency / World Factbook mirror
pages_or_sections: country profile; lines 71-72 in tool view
strengths: Provides a near-contemporaneous FRY population estimate of 11,206,847 and explicitly warns that war-related dislocation affects precision.
limitations: Mirror copy and national-level estimate; not a daily wartime stock series.
notes: Used as the civilian-stock baseline for the campaign-level denominator.

title: Under Orders in Kosovo - part_two.pdf
strengths: Documents the expulsion of 862,979 ethnic Albanians and broader mass displacement during the period of NATO bombing.
limitations: Displacement is not converted into a daily FRY-wide civilian stock series.
notes: Used only to bound the average civilian exposure denominator.
